segments.label: Topic frame
segments.narrationText: How Public Policy Shapes Markets looks simple on the surface, but it is really a system of incentives, rules, and feedback loops.
segments.subtext: How How Public Policy Shapes Markets actually functions.
segments.visualDirection: Open on people and motion to make the topic feel immediate and human.

segments.label: Hook
segments.narrationText: This matters because how public policy shapes markets changes what people can afford, access, or influence in everyday life.
segments.subtext: The critical vulnerabilities hidden within How Public Policy Shapes Markets.
segments.visualDirection: Use a dense icon field that quickly frames the pressure point or conflict.

segments.label: System boundary
segments.narrationText: To understand it, start by mapping the main actors, the rules they follow, and the limits of the system.
segments.subtext: Where does the jurisdiction of this system begin and end?
segments.visualDirection: Show the system as connected nodes so the audience sees where the edges are.

segments.label: Cause layer 1
segments.narrationText: The first layer is the core driver that keeps how public policy shapes markets moving even when the outcomes look unfair or inefficient.
segments.subtext: The primary engine driving How Public Policy Shapes Markets.
segments.visualDirection: Present the first causal layer as a simple abstract mechanism or formula.

segments.label: Cause layer 2
segments.narrationText: Then that driver flows through institutions, prices, behavior, and public expectations.
segments.subtext: How these mechanisms propagate through the network.
segments.visualDirection: Turn the mechanism into directional movement so cause and effect read instantly.

segments.label: Cause layer 3
segments.narrationText: Over time, feedback loops reinforce the pattern and make the system harder to change.
segments.subtext: Complex interactions emerging at the deepest layer.
segments.visualDirection: Reveal the deeper feedback structure with denser motion and higher visual complexity.

segments.label: Data lens
segments.narrationText: The data lens helps separate intuition from measurement and shows which pressures are actually growing.
segments.subtext: What the raw metrics reveal about How Public Policy Shapes Markets.
segments.visualDirection: Shift to a data-centric visual that feels analytical and system-wide.

segments.label: Real world scene
segments.narrationText: What looks abstract in theory appears in the real world as daily constraints, tradeoffs, and visible pressure points.
segments.subtext: How policy translates into physical execution.
segments.visualDirection: Bring the abstract explanation back to a concrete everyday environment.

segments.label: Ecology/externalities
segments.narrationText: Every system pushes some costs outward, and those hidden externalities are often what people notice last.
segments.subtext: The hidden costs externalized by the system.
segments.visualDirection: Show hidden side effects with ecological or externality imagery.

segments.label: Macro trend
segments.narrationText: At a larger scale, the same forces shape long-term trends across regions, industries, and institutions.
segments.subtext: Where this trajectory implies we are headed next.
segments.visualDirection: Zoom outward and frame the topic as part of a wider long-range pattern.

segments.label: Actionable takeaway
segments.narrationText: The practical takeaway is to focus on leverage points where small rule changes or behavior changes alter the whole system.
segments.subtext: Leveraging this architecture for positive outcomes.
segments.visualDirection: Return to a clear icon-driven summary that points toward action.

segments.label: Closing
segments.narrationText: Once you see how how public policy shapes markets works as a system, the outcomes stop looking random.
segments.visualDirection: Close on people again so the system explanation resolves back to human consequence.
